temporal_description: A medium shot shows two men in a hallway. The man on the left, with dark hair, wears a pink and white plaid shirt and dark pants. He is in motion, walking towards the right. The man on the right, also with dark hair, is dressed in a blue plaid shirt and light-colored pants. He stands in a doorway, looking at the first man with a concerned expression. The background consists of light-colored walls adorned with framed pictures and a dark wooden door with the number "23" on it. The camera pans slightly to the right, following the men as they move into a living room. The man in the blue shirt gestures with his hand as he speaks to the man in the pink shirt, who is now seen from the back. The room is furnished with a couch, a lamp, and more framed art on the walls. White text with a black outline appears on the screen, reading, "EXECUTIVE PRODUCER SHANA GOLDBERG-MEEHAN." The camera cuts to a closer, over-the-shoulder shot from behind the man in the pink shirt. The man in the blue shirt is now in the foreground, looking down with a serious expression. The living room setting, including a window with curtains and a wall sconce, is visible behind him. A man speaks in a slightly exasperated and questioning tone, his voice filled with disbelief as he asks, "Soaps, shampoos? Are you really taking all this stuff?" The background is quiet, with only the faint, ambient sound of a room, suggesting an indoor conversation. The camera angle shifts to a medium close-up of the man in the pink shirt. He is now facing the camera, looking down with a shocked and slightly distressed expression, his mouth open as if speaking. The living room, including a couch and a lamp, is visible in the soft-focus background.
